title: Apparatus and method for producing and dispensing a reaction mixture
abstract: The apparatus comprises a mixing head (5) for mixing the reaction components that are fed via two supply lines (7, 8) to the mixing head (5) under the pressure which prevails in the containers (1, 2). The apparatus comprises a decompression system to relieve the pressure from the supply lines (7, 8) to avoid the reaction components seeping into the mixing head (5). The decompression system comprises two pressure vessels (13, 17) each with two sides, separated by a membrane (27). One of these sides forms an expansion chamber (25, 26) for the reaction component while the other side (29, 30) is connected to a pneumatic system (31, 33, 44, 45, 46). With this pneumatic system, the expansion chamber (25, 26) can be pressurized before the production of the reaction mixture is started. After the production of the reaction mixture, the pressure can be relieved again from the expansion chamber (25, 26) and thus from the supply line (7, 8). Such a decompression system is simple and easy to maintain. The pressure vessels (13, 17) are furthermore detachable, as a result of which they can be easily replaced during maintenance.
claims:
Claims 1.An apparatus for producing and dispensing a reaction mixture starting from at least a first and a second reaction component that are under pressure in a first (1 ) and a second container (2) respectively, which apparatus comprises: - a mixing head (5) for mutually mixing said first and said second reaction component; which mixing head (5) is provided with an outlet (6) for the mixture of both reaction components; - a first supply line (7) for feeding the first reaction component from said first container (1 ) under the pressure prevailing therein to the mixing head (5); - a second supply line (8) for feeding the second reaction component from said second container (2) under the pressure prevailing therein to the mixing head (5); - a first shut-off valve (12) in said first supply line (7) and a second shut-off valve (16) in said second supply line (8), the first (12) and the second shut-off valve (16) each having an open and a closed position; - a third shut-off valve (18) for shutting off the first supply line (7) in said mixing head (5) and a fourth shut-off valve (19) for shutting off the second supply line (8) in said mixing head (5), the third (18) and the fourth shut-off valve (19) comprising a common plunger which is configured to slide, in particular in its longitudinal direction, for opening and closing the third (18) and the fourth shut-off valve (19) simultaneously; - a first expansion chamber (25) which is connected to the first supply line (7) between said first (12) and said third shut-off valve (18); - a second expansion chamber (26) which is connected to the second supply line (8) between said second (16) and said fourth shut-off valve (19); - a pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46 or 31 , 44, 45) for the pressurisation and pressure-relief of the first expansion chamber (25) and of said first supply line (7) as from said first (12) to said third shut-off valve (18), 

 and for the pressurisation and pressure-relief of the second expansion chamber (26) and of said second supply line (8) as from said second (16) to said fourth shut-off valve (19); and - a control system (20) for operating said pneumatic system and said first (12), second (16), third (18) and fourth shut-off valves (19) for starting and stopping the production and dispensing of said reaction mixture, characterised in that said first (25) and said second (26) expansion chamber, respectively are formed by a first (13) and a second pressure vessel (17) having each two sides separated from each other by a membrane (27), of which a first side forms the first (25) respectively the second (26) expansion chamber, arranged to be filled with said first and respectively said second reaction component, and of which a second side (29, 30) is connected to said pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46 or 31 , 44, 45) to be filled with a gas, which gas is in contact through said membrane (27) with the reaction component in the first side (25, 26) of the pressure vessel (13, 17), said first (13) and said second pressure vessel (17) being detachably connected to said pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46 of 31 , 44, 45) and to the first (7) and the second (8) supply line respectively. 2. An apparatus according to claim 1 , characterised in that said pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46 or 31 , 44, 45) is provided to bring the first expansion chamber (25) under a pressure which is higher than said pressure in the first container (1 ) and to bring the second expansion chamber (26) under a pressure which is higher than said pressure in the second container (2). 3. An apparatus according to claim 1 or 2, characterised in that said control (20) is provided with a manual or automatic actuator (9) for starting and stopping the production and dispensing of the reaction mixture. 4. An apparatus according to any one of claims 1 to 3, characterised in that said control (20) is configured to pressurise said first (25) and second (26) expansion chambers by means of said pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46 or 31 , 44, 45) before opening said first (12) and said second shut-off valve (16). 5. An apparatus according to claim 4, characterised in that said control (20) is configured to open the third (18) and the fourth (19) shut-off valve after having opened said first (12) and second shut-off valve (16). 6. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 5, characterised in that said control (20) is configured to close said third (18) and said fourth (19) shut-off valve again after they were opened. 7. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 6, characterised in that said control (20) is configured to relieve the pressure from said first (25) and second (26) expansion chambers after said third (18) and fourth (19) shut-off valves are closed. 8. An apparatus according to claim 7, characterised in that said control (20) is configured to relieve the pressure from said first (25) and second (26) expansion chamber only after a predetermined period of time after having closed the third (18) and fourth (19) shut-off valves, more particularly after a predetermined period of time of at least 5 seconds, preferably after a predetermined period of time of at least 10 seconds. 9. An apparatus according to claim 7 or 8, characterised in that said control (20) is configured to close said first (12) and said second shut-off valve (16) before the pressure is released from said first (25) and said second expansion chamber (26). 10. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 9, characterised in that said pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46 or 31 , 44, 45) is provided to bring the pressure of the gas in the second side (29, 30) of the first (13) and the second pressure vessel (17) substantially to atmospheric pressure when releasing the pressure from the first (25) and the second expansion chamber (26). 11. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 10, characterised in that the first (13) and the second pressure vessel (17) is provided with a support surface (35) for limiting the movement of said membrane (27) in the direction of respectively said first (25) and said second 

 expansion chamber (26), the support surface (35) being preferably provided for substantially completely supporting the membrane (27). 12. An apparatus according to claim 11 , characterised in that said control (20) is configured to open said third (18) and said fourth valve (19) after the membrane (27) of the first (13) and the second (17) pressure vessel has been pressed by the first (31 , 44, 45) and the second pneumatic system (33, 44, 46) against said support surface (35). 13. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 12, characterised in that said membrane (27) is convex, the convex side of the membrane (27) being directed respectively towards the first (25) and the second expansion chamber (26). 14. An apparatus according to claim 13, characterised in that said membrane (27) remains convex when the pressure is relieved from the first (25) and the second (26) expansion chamber. 15. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 14, characterised in that said membrane (27) is bladder-shaped, the first (25) and the second expansion chamber (26) being located outside the bladder shape. 16. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 15, characterised in that said membrane (27) is made from a fluoropolymer elastomeric material. 17. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 16, characterised in that a first and a second float chamber (40), containing a first and a second float (41 ) respectively, are provided respectively in said first supply line (7), between said first shut-off valve (12) and said first container (1 ), and in said second supply line (8), between said second shut-off valve (16) and said second container (2), the first and the second float (41 ) being provided to detect the level of respectively the first and the second reaction component in the first and the second float chamber (40), said first and said second float chamber (40) having preferably an internal volume which is larger than 2 litres, preferably larger than 3 litres and more preferably larger than 4 litres. 18. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 17, characterised in that a first (11 ) and a second filter (15) are provided respectively in said first supply line (7), between said first valve (12) and said first container (1 ), and in said second supply line (8), between said second valve (16) and said second container (2), which filters are located respectively in a first and in a second housing (36). 19. An apparatus according to claims 17 and 18, characterised in that said first and said second housing (36) respectively also form said first and said second float chamber (40). 20. An apparatus according to claims 17 and 18, characterised in that said first float chamber (40) is located in the first supply line (7) between the first filter (11 ) and the first container (1 ), and in that said second float chamber (40) is located in the second supply line (8) between the second filter (15) and the second container (2), the first (11 ) and the second filter (15) being preferably provided with a venting system (42). 21. An apparatus according to any of the claims 18 to 20, characterised in that the housing (36) of the first filter (11 ) and the housing (36) of the second filter (15) are provided with heating elements (43) to heat respectively the first and the second reaction component in the first (11 ) and the second filter (15). 22. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 21 , characterised in that said first (1 ) and said second container (2) are arranged to be brought under a pressure of 5 to 10 bars, preferably under a pressure of 6 to 8 bars. 23. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 22, characterised in that said pneumatic system (31 , 33, 44, 45, 46) comprises a first pneumatic system (31 , 44, 45) for the pressurisation of and the pressure relief from the first expansion chamber (25) and from said first supply line (7) as from said first (12) to said third valve (18) and a second pneumatic system (33, 44, 46) for the pressurisation of and pressure relief from the second 

 expansion chamber (26) and from said second supply line (8) as from said second (16) to said fourth valve (19). 24. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 22, characterised in that said pneumatic system (31 , 44, 45) comprising a common pneumatic system (31 , 44, 45) for the pressurisation of and the pressure relief from the first (25) and the second expansion chamber (26) and said first supply line (7) as from said first (12) to said third valve (18) and said second supply line (8) as from said second (16) to said fourth shut-off valve (19). 25. An apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 24, characterised in that it comprises said first container (1 ), which is filled with the first reaction component, and said second container (2), which is filled with the second reaction component, said first supply line (7) being connected to the first container (1 ) and said second supply line (8) being connected to the second container (2). 26. A method for producing and dispensing a reaction mixture with an apparatus according to any one of the claims 1 to 25, in which method said first supply line (7) is connected to said first container (1 ) which is filled with the first reaction component, and said second supply line (8) is connected to said second container (2) which is filled with the second reaction component, after which the first (1 ) and the second container (2) are pressurised. 27. A method according to claim 26, characterised in that said first container (1 ) upon being empty, is replaced by a filled first container (1 ) and said second container (2) upon being empty, is replaced by a filled second container (2). 28. Use of a set of a first container and a second container for carrying out a method according to claim 26 or 27, the reaction mixture being a polyurethane reaction mixture containing no or insufficient physical blowing agents, preferably less than 4% by weight and more preferably less than 2% by weight, to be dispensed in the form of a froth and the first and the second container being arranged to be pressurised, in particular under a pressure of at least 5 bars. 29. Use according to claim 28, characterised in that the first container contains an isocyanate reaction component and the second container a polyol reaction component, which polyol reaction component mainly contains water as blowing agent for producing a polyurethane foam. 30. Use according to claim 29, characterised in that the polyol reaction component is free of physical blowing agents.
